content.cn_text: 第三部分。你将听到两名运动物理治疗专业的学生 John 和 Cath 正在准备
content.en_text: Section 3. You will hear two sports physiotherapy students, called John and Cath, preparing

content.cn_text: 一个关于运动员足部损伤的联合展示。首先，你有一些时间
content.en_text: a joint presentation on foot injuries in sports players. First, you have some time to

content.cn_text: 浏览第4页第21至第26题。
content.en_text: look at questions 21 to 26 on page 4.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第26题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 26.

content.cn_text: John：Cath，我看了你的展示计划草稿，并和我的比较了一下，我们
content.en_text: John: I've had a look at your draft presentation plan and compared it with mine, Cath, and we

content.cn_text: 似乎有相同的基本结构。我们都包含了相同的三个主要部分，
content.en_text: seem to have the same basic structure. We've both included the same three main sections,

content.cn_text: 也就是足部解剖、损伤和治疗，而且我们组织
content.en_text: that's the anatomy of the foot, the injuries and the treatment, and we've organised our

content.cn_text: 展示的方式也一样，但我们选择用来说明可能损伤的运动类型
content.en_text: presentations in the same way, but the types of sports we've chosen to illustrate the

content.cn_text: 不一样。
content.en_text: possible injuries aren't the same.

content.cn_text: Cath：我来处理解剖部分。我有 Gilligan 教授讲座上的笔记和讲义。
content.en_text: Cath: I'll deal with the anatomy bit. I've got notes and a handout on that from Professor
Gilligan's lecture.

content.cn_text: John：哦，我本来想做那部分。我下载了一些有用资料。我确实觉得我们应该限制
content.en_text: John: Oh, I was hoping to do that. I've downloaded some useful stuff. I do think we should limit

content.cn_text: 这一部分。主要重点是治疗时，我们不想在解剖上花太多时间。
content.en_text: this section though. We don't want to spend much time on anatomy when the main focus is
on the treatment.

content.cn_text: Cath：是的，不过在最后一部分，也许我们两个人可以展示这些
content.en_text: Cath: That's true, but in the last section, maybe the two of us could show how some of these

content.cn_text: 疗法实际如何操作。我可以当病人，你来做治疗。
content.en_text: therapies actually work. I could be the patient and you could do the treatment.

content.cn_text: John：嗯，好主意，因为损伤大多是内部的，所以很难找到图片。
content.en_text: John: Hmm, good idea, because it's hard to find pictures as injuries are mostly internal.

content.cn_text: 听受伤运动员的第一手叙述也会很好。
content.en_text: It would be good to hear some first-hand accounts from injured athletes too.

content.cn_text: Cath：是的，但我们没有时间。
content.en_text: Cath: Yeah, but we don't have time.

content.cn_text: John：没有。那我们要不要讨论不同类型的脚跟损伤？值得指出的是，
content.en_text: John: No. Should we then discuss different types of heel injury? It's worth pointing out that

content.cn_text: 它们在严重程度上差异很大。可能只是肌肉拉伤，也可能是骨骼
content.en_text: these vary enormously in how bad they are. It might be just a pulled muscle, or a bone

content.cn_text: 损伤，需要手术。有时只有专家才能分辨差异。
content.en_text: injury, which needs surgery. Sometimes only an expert can tell the difference.

content.cn_text: Cath：很好。我来做这部分幻灯片。这个交给我。
content.en_text: Cath: Excellent. I'll do the slides on that. Leave that one to me.

content.cn_text: John: 那脚跟损伤的原因呢？
content.en_text: John: Now, what about causes of heel injuries?

content.cn_text: Cath：列出来很容易，比如过度使用、不合适的鞋。
content.en_text: Cath：Well, it's easy enough to list them: overuse, improper footwear.

content.cn_text: John: 是的，还有错误的跑步方式。
content.en_text: John: Yes. Incorrect running style.

content.cn_text: Cath：这些都相关，但为了简单起见，我们就选一个原因。比如关于
content.en_text: Cath：They're all relevant, but for simplicity, let's just pick one cause. Say, stuff about

content.cn_text: 突然改变训练习惯的内容。
content.en_text: changing your training habit suddenly.

content.cn_text: John: 你是说，比如突然做很多山地训练？ 是的。这个主意不错。
content.en_text: John: You mean, like, suddenly doing a lot of mountain training? Yes. That's a good idea.

content.cn_text: 而且最新一期Physio Journal 上有这方面研究，对吧？
content.en_text: And there was research on that in the latest edition of Physio Journal, wasn't there?

content.cn_text: Gilligan 教授推荐给我们了。
content.en_text: Professor Gilligan recommended it to us.

content.cn_text: Cath：好。拉伸技术这部分可以交给我。我真的很喜欢这类内容。
content.en_text: Cath: Great. You can leave the bit on stretching techniques to me. I really like all that stuff.

content.cn_text: John：那你打算怎么讲？
content.en_text: John: So what's going to be your approach there?

content.cn_text: Cath：我会用视觉资料总结各种技术，然后提出
content.en_text: Cath: Well, I'll summarize all the various techniques with visual support, and then I'll make the

content.cn_text: 拉伸应该被谨慎管理这一点。
content.en_text: point that the stretching should be carefully managed.

content.cn_text: 显然，你必须小心不要过度拉伸，而且只能在肌肉
content.en_text: Obviously, you have to be careful not to over-stretch, and to stretch only when your muscles

content.cn_text: 热身后再拉伸。否则，你可能会弊大于利。
content.en_text: are warmed up. Otherwise, you could do yourself more harm than good.

content.cn_text: 在有监督的项目中，之后把拉伸和
content.en_text: It's also important that on a supervised programme, stretching is later on combined with

content.cn_text: 平衡练习结合也很重要，这些练习既能拉伸也能强化肌肉。
content.en_text: balance exercises, which stretch and also strengthen the muscles.

content.cn_text: John：好的。
content.en_text: John: Okay, then.

content.cn_text: 在听剩余讨论之前，你有一些时间浏览第27题
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 27

content.cn_text: 到第30题，位于第5页。
content.en_text: to 30 on page 5.

content.cn_text: 现在请听录音，并回答第27至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 27 to 30.

content.cn_text: John：最后准备一些案例研究信息总是有用的，以防我们
content.en_text: John: It's always useful to have some information on case studies at the end, in case we're

content.cn_text: 材料不够。你找到什么了吗？
content.en_text: short of material. Did you manage to find anything?

content.cn_text: Cath：找到了。一位30多岁的女跑者谈到她治疗脚跟肿胀的经历。一开始，
content.en_text: Cath: Yes. A female runner in her 30s talking about her treatment for a swollen heel. Initially,

content.cn_text: 她让伤处休息。这确实帮助减轻了肿胀，虽然仍然有点
content.en_text: she rested the injury. This did help to reduce the swelling, although it was still a little

content.cn_text: 不舒服。然后，她每天两次给伤处敷冰袋。
content.en_text: bit uncomfortable. Then, she applied an ice pack to the injury twice a day.

content.cn_text: John：那有效吗？
content.en_text: John: Did that work?

content.cn_text: Cath：她坚持这种治疗一个月，但最后认为它没有帮助。
content.en_text: Cath: Well, she persisted with this treatment for the first month, but decided it wasn't helping.

content.cn_text: 之后，她学会了如何使用深层组织按摩技术，并且很惊讶
content.en_text: After this period, she was shown how to use deep tissue massage techniques, and was surprised

content.cn_text: 这种方法让她的脚活动自如了许多。
content.en_text: at how much freedom of movement this created in her foot.

content.cn_text: John：她不是还在接受超声波治疗吗？
content.en_text: John: Wasn't she having ultrasound treatment?

content.cn_text: Cath：是的，她在医院接受治疗，但她仍然不相信它有价值。
content.en_text: Cath: Yes, she was having sessions at the hospital, but she remained unconvinced about its value.

content.cn_text: 她对医生开给她的平衡练习也有同样感觉。
content.en_text: She felt the same about the balancing exercises she was prescribed.

content.cn_text: John：好的，这很有意思。我看了一名男短跑运动员的后续护理计划，他被允许
content.en_text: John: Okay, that's interesting. I looked at the aftercare programme of a male sprinter who was allowed

content.cn_text: 在康复过程中做一些体育活动。经过四个月治疗后，他做了
content.en_text: to do some sports activity as part of his rehabilitation. After four months of treatment, he did

content.cn_text: 一些草地轻跑，穿着能给受伤脚跟提供充分支撑的鞋。
content.en_text: some light running on grass, wearing shoes that gave plenty of support to his injured
heel.

content.cn_text: Cath：所以不疼吗？
content.en_text: Cath: So, it didn't hurt?

content.cn_text: John：嗯，他发现之后脚跟会疼，一些跳跃练习也是一样。
content.en_text: John: Well, he found the heel was sore afterwards, and the same happened with some jumping exercises

content.cn_text: 他被要求尝试这些练习。
content.en_text: he was asked to try.

content.cn_text: Cath：他去健身房做重量训练了吗？
content.en_text: Cath: Did he go to the gym and use weights?

content.cn_text: John：是的，几周后去了。而且如果他用轻重量，就可以无痛训练。
content.en_text: John: Yes, after a few weeks. And if he used light weights, he could train without pain.

content.cn_text: 他还尝试了骑自行车，发现这很有益，因为他的脚不会接触地面。
content.en_text: He also tried cycling and found this beneficial as his foot was not making contact with the ground.

content.cn_text: 他原本以为游泳也会有类似效果，但发现反复踢腿动作
content.en_text: He expected a similar result with swimming, but found the repetitive kicking motion was

content.cn_text: 实际上加重了伤情。
content.en_text: actually aggravating the injury.

content.cn_text: Cath：嗯，这两个案例研究都有价值。
content.en_text: Cath: Well, both these case studies have value.

content.cn_text: 第三部分到此结束。你现在有半分钟时间检查答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of section three. You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 现在请翻到第6页第四部分。
content.en_text: Now, turn to section four on page six.

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C
groups.title: Questions 21–26

items.title: John and Cath’s presentation plans are different in
items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题问 John 和 Cath 的展示计划哪里不同。原文说结构、三大部分和组织方式都相同，但选择说明损伤的 sports types 不同，所以答案是 A. the examples used。

items.title: What do the students agree about the anatomy section of their presentation?

options.title: A
options.content: It would be better if Cath spoke about it.

options.title: B
options.content: It should be kept quite short.

options.title: C
options.content: It should be based on information from the internet.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第22题问两人对展示中 anatomy 部分达成什么一致。原文说 should limit this section，不想在 anatomy 上花太多时间，所以答案是 B. It should be kept quite short。

items.title: What do the students agree to include in the last section?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题问两人同意在最后一部分加入什么。原文提议 show how therapies actually work，并说一个当病人一个做治疗，即治疗演示，所以答案是 B. demonstrations of treatment。pictures 和 patient accounts 都没有采用。

items.title: What is said about the different types of heel injury?

options.title: C
options.content: Some are more serious than others.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第24题问不同类型的脚跟损伤有什么特点。原文说 vary enormously in how bad they are，有的只是肌肉拉伤，有的骨伤需要手术，所以答案是 C. Some are more serious than others。

items.title: On the subject of causes of heel injuries, the students agree to

options.title: C
options.content: use a source written by their professor.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问关于脚跟损伤原因，两人同意做什么。原文说 for simplicity, let's just pick one cause，所以答案是 A. focus on a single reason。Physio Journal 是资料来源，不是他们教授写的。

items.title: What does Cath say about stretching as a treatment?

options.title: B
options.content: It is commonly confused with strengthening.

options.title: C
options.content: It is the least effective part of treatment.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问 Cath 对拉伸作为治疗方式怎么说。原文说 stretching should be carefully managed，否则可能 more harm than good，说明有潜在风险，所以答案是 A. It is potentially risky。

groups.desc:
Choose TWO letters, A–E.
Which TWO treatment techniques did the female runner find useful for her swollen heel?
groups.title: Questions 27 and 28

items.title: Which TWO treatment techniques did the female runner find useful for her swollen heel?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题为多选题，问女跑者觉得有用的治疗技术之一。原文说 rested the injury did help to reduce the swelling，所以答案包含 C. rest。ice pack 后文说没帮助，是干扰。

items.title: Which TWO treatment techniques did the female runner find useful for her swollen heel?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题为多选题，问女跑者觉得有用的另一项治疗技术。原文说 deep tissue massage 让她脚部活动自由度增加，所以答案包含 A. massage。

groups.desc:
Choose TWO letters, A–E.
Which TWO sports did the male sprinter find most effective during his rehabilitation programme?
groups.title: Questions 29 and 30

items.title: Which TWO sports did the male sprinter find most effective during his rehabilitation programme?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题为多选题，问男短跑运动员康复过程中最有效的运动之一。原文说使用 light weights 时可以 without pain，所以答案包含 B. weight-training。running on grass 和 jumping 会疼，是干扰。

items.title: Which TWO sports did the male sprinter find most effective during his rehabilitation programme?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题为多选题，问男短跑运动员康复过程中最有效的另一项运动。原文说 cycling beneficial，因为脚不接触地面，所以答案包含 D. cycling。

source_label: 🍠@Listening 永不虾滑 · 仅供个人学习
source_credit: 此内容由🍠@Listening 永不虾滑整理，仅作为分享供个人使用；禁止商用、倒卖和引流。有问题请先仔细看🍠这个账号的置顶和分享贴。没有团队，不是机构，纯个人分享。督学和其他相关课程请咨询主页另一个号@心情有点虾滑（不回任何机经相关消息）。
